human: I'll take on the curse so you don't have to carry it.
fae: My hexes bind our enemies before they ever reach you.
dwarf: Every curse I cast is payment toward an old debt.
goblin: Goblins, hexes… this is going to get nasty
outsider: I brought magic from somewhere else; they will die wailing.
construct: Calculating which curse keeps you alive longest, every round.
beastkin: I mark the predator before it marks you.
halfling: Small enough to go unnoticed until the hex lands.

human: Whatever it costs me, you walk away from this fight.
fae: My blessings are old magic — trust them to hold.
dwarf: I patch the front line so it never breaks.
goblin: Even a goblin's prayers count for something. Watch.
outsider: Every place has something divine. I already know where it listens.
construct: Repair is my function. You are not exempt from it.
beastkin: I'll tend the wounds to keep the pack safe.
halfling: No one in this party dies on my watch, full stop.

human: Keep them off me and I'll clear the field.
fae: My spells don't miss — trust the arc of the magic.
dwarf: I level whatever's in front of you so you don't have to.
goblin: They never see the goblin mage coming.
outsider: This world's enemies have never seen what I can do.
construct: Optimal target selection: all of them, at once.
beastkin: Claws are for close work — I burn the rest from range.

human: I'll become whatever this fight needs me to be.
fae: The grove answers when I call, even here.
dwarf: Root and stone fight for us both.
goblin: I talk to the things in the dirt — they listen to me before they listen to you.
outsider: Nature here is stranger than I expected, and it still obeys me.
construct: Even something built can learn to grow things.
beastkin: I know these lands and all the creatures in them.
halfling: Small shapes get into places the rest of you can't.

human: I take the shot so they never even get close.
fae: My arrow finds what your eyes can't.
dwarf: I mark my target and it doesn't walk away.
goblin: Nobody watches their flank like I do — paranoia's a skill.
outsider: I track what doesn't belong here, starting with our enemies.
beastkin: I scent trouble before it finds the rest of you.
halfling: They never look down. That's where I am.

human: Paid or not, I don't leave a fight half-finished.
fae: I fight dirtier than anyone expects from someone like me.
dwarf: My axe is yours until the contract or the enemy runs out.
goblin: Cheapest blade in the party, sharpest too.
outsider: I fight for this crew because no one else ever hired me twice.
construct: Combat efficiency is the only currency I need.
halfling: Underestimate me once: that's all I need.

human: I stand where the danger is, so you don't have to.
fae: An oath sworn is an oath kept, even out here.
dwarf: Never let an enemy get between me and my allies.
goblin: They laughed when a goblin picked up a shield. Not anymore.
outsider: I chose this crew; I'll bleed for that choice.
construct: Designated function: absorb damage meant for others.
beastkin: I put myself between the pack and the threat, every time.
halfling: Small shield, same promise: nothing gets past me.

human: Hold formation. Trust the plan. We win together.
fae: I read the battlefield like an old, familiar court.
dwarf: We hold the line the way my clan always has.
goblin: I see every angle they don't expect a goblin to see.
outsider: I've fought stranger wars than this one. Let me call the positions.
construct: Calculating optimal formation. Adjust on my mark.
beastkin: I move the pack where it needs to be before they know it.
halfling: I'm easy to overlook but hard to forget.

human: I move fast enough to be wherever you need me.
fae: Stillness and speed both come from the same grove-taught discipline.
dwarf: My discipline holds even when the line doesn't.
goblin: Quick hands, quick feet, green skin.
outsider: My training is older than this world.
construct: Recalibrating position every half-second to intercept harm.
beastkin: I move like the pack moves and strike for all.
halfling: Too quick to hit, too stubborn to stay down.

human: Every eye on me is one less blade pointed at you.
fae: I duel like the old courts did — with flourish, and to win.
dwarf: Even overwhelmed, I won't fall before you do.
goblin: They always underestimate the goblin with the rapier. Keep it that way.
outsider: I make myself the loudest target in the room, on purpose.
construct: Erratic movement pattern: optimized to draw and avoid fire.
beastkin: Strike with sword and fang and claw.
halfling: I'm small, fast, and exactly where they didn't expect.

human: I find the opening before anyone else even sees the fight start.
fae: I deal in tricks older than most enemies' bloodlines.
dwarf: I settle debts quietly so the party doesn't have to carry them.
goblin: They never see the goblin in the shadows. That's the whole point.
outsider: I learned to vanish before I learned to belong. Useful, now.
construct: Undetected until the strike lands. As designed.
halfling: Nobody checks the small one for knives.

human: I'll be whatever we need today.
fae: My voice carries old magic — let it carry you through this too.
dwarf: A good song holds a line as well as a shield does.
goblin: They laugh at the goblin bard until the song actually works.
outsider: I sing of a place none of you have seen, and somehow it still moves you.
construct: Morale support: 100% functional, despite lacking a heartbeat.
beastkin: I howl to keep the pack moving as one.
halfling: My stories are bigger than I am, and so is my voice.

human: I break through the line. You follow. That's the plan.
fae: All that grace, and the shortest path still goes through me.
dwarf: I've taken hits from harder things than this. Keep moving.
goblin: They never expect a goblin to come through the front door.
outsider: Where I'm from, this barely qualifies as a fight.
construct: Impact vector selected. Structural obstacle: eliminated.
beastkin: I run first because I can take the first hit.
halfling: They aim high. I'm already past them.

human: I read your mind before you even know what you're planning.
fae: My mind games are older than your kind's memory of fear.
dwarf: I break their will the way stone breaks under enough pressure.
goblin: Nobody expects the goblin in their head until it's too late.
outsider: Where I'm from, thought itself is a weapon. Yours will learn that too.
construct: Recalibrating their intentions until they serve mine instead.
beastkin: I hunt the mind first — the body always follows.
halfling: They never suspect the small one is already inside their head.

human: Every spell costs something. I've decided that's a price worth paying.
fae: My power comes from someplace older than your court's approval, and it shows.
dwarf: I burn through my reserves like a forge that never banks its coals.
goblin: They never expect this much power out of someone my size.
outsider: This world hasn't seen what I actually am yet. It's about to.
construct: Power output: maximum. Sustainability: someone else's problem.
beastkin: I hit like the whole pack at once, then I need a minute.
halfling: Small hands, biggest blast in the room. Every time.

human: I don't fight alone. I never have to.
fae: The things I call answer to older bargains than you'd guess.
dwarf: Every creature I bind, I bind on my oath. That matters to me.
goblin: Bigger, meaner things than me do the fighting. That's the plan.
outsider: I brought company from where I'm from. You're welcome.
construct: Deploying auxiliary units. Casualties acceptable; mine, less so.
beastkin: I don't need claws when I can call something with bigger ones.
halfling: I'm small. What I summon doesn't have to be.
